response:
Sec. 1  
people allergic to peanuts cannot live underwater because their bodies are not adapted to the pressure changes in water  
the human body is designed for air and not for water which causes the lungs to expand and the heart to weaken  
allergies to peanuts are triggered by proteins in the food not by the environment  
the immune system of allergic individuals reacts to the peanuts rather than to the water  
the pressure in water affects the body differently than in air  
the body's tissues and organs are not equipped to handle the increased pressure in water  
the lungs cannot expand properly in water which makes breathing difficult  
the heart cannot pump blood efficiently in water which causes dizziness and fainting  
the body's systems are not adapted to the unique conditions of underwater environments  

Sec. 2  
the lack of oxygen in water makes it difficult for the body to function  
the temperature in water is different from that in air which affects the body's metabolism  
the absence of sunlight in water affects the body's circadian rhythm  
the body's ability to regulate temperature is different in water  
the body's ability to maintain homeostasis is different in water  
the body's ability to handle the physical demands of underwater living is limited  
the body's ability to handle the psychological stress of underwater living is limited  
the body's ability to handle the physical and psychological demands of underwater living is limited
